subject: [CCSDK-92] summary
commit_message:
[CCSDK-92] summary

Changes made:
1. removed hardcoded JDBC driver's name and made it a property.
2. Optimized connection pool initialization
3. Removed unused factories.
4. In DblibJREFileResolver checked for null value of bundle variable.

Change-Id: I86a3a1355ba453a4094c5714be4530915e618947
Signed-off-by: Rich Tabedzki <richard.tabedzki@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Comply with Commit best practices at https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Commit+Messages
Do not include the Jira-id in the 50 chars header but in external reference section
Use this cmd to amend the msg: git commit -s --amend

Also, consider submitting only 1 change per commit.

Patch Set 2:

Rich, in this case to amend commit message the process would be as follows:

1. Checkout change (click on download in upper right hand of this window, copy the 'checkout' method): "git fetch http://gerrit.onap.org/r/ccsdk/sli/core refs/changes/11/12311/2 && git checkout FETCH_HEAD" or use git review "git review -d 12311"
2. Amend Change "git commit --amend" - Change commit message, save.
3. Check Commit "git log -1"
4. Upload Change "git push ssh://<user-name>@gerrit.onap.org:29418/ccsdk/sli/core HEAD:refs/for/master" or use git review "git review"

Patch Set 2:

To rebase change to fix merge conflict the process with git review is:

1. Checkout master "git checkout master" 
2. Fetch remote changes for Origin Master "git fetch origin master"
3. Pull remote changes "git pull master"
4. Checkout this gerrit change "git review -d 12311" (I believe git review creates a branch but if not "git checkout -b <branch-name>)
5. Rebase code on master "git rebase master <branch-name>"
6. When rebase complains about merge conflicts you will need to go into code and choose changes to keep. You can see files concisely by doing "git rebase --continue" again.
7. Once files are fixed, add them "git add <filename> <filename>"
8. Continue Rebase "git rebase --continue"
9. Push rebased patch "git review"

Patch Set 2:

It looks like there's a merge conflict now on this.  Marcus - to clear this, can Rich just do a git pull (which should do a merge), resolve the conflicts, then do a git commit --amend and git review -R?

Abandoned

Abandoning this change do to restrictions imposed
